response:
Fallback Path Evidence:

In the context of quantized GPU workloads, kernel dispatch evidence is a crucial factor that determines the performance of the application. In this context, fallback path evidence refers to the alternative paths that can be taken by the application when it encounters a specific kernel. This evidence helps in identifying the most efficient path for the application to follow, thereby improving its performance.

Kernel Dispatch Evidence:

In contrast, kernel dispatch evidence is a set of instructions that are executed by the GPU during a specific kernel execution. In this context, direct rocBLAS/Teensile dispatch naming can remain hidden in quantized GGUF workloads because it does not provide any fallback path evidence. This means that the application will always execute the same kernel with the same parameters regardless of its chosen dispatch path.

Why Direct RocBLAS/Teensile Dispatch Naming Can Remain Hidden:

Direct rocBLAS/Teensile dispatch naming is a crucial factor in quantized GGUF workloads because it provides fallback path evidence. Fallback path evidence helps in identifying the most efficient path for the application to follow,
